name: Ochagavía - Lakuaga Loop Trail
name_original: Vuelta desde Ochagavía a la zona sur de los Pirineos Navarros
description: This difficult 25.9-mile mountain biking route from Ochagavía in the Navarrese Pyrenees offers stunning views and dense forests.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Ochagavía/Otsagabia offers diverse terrain within the Navarrese Pyrenees and the Salazar Valley. The region is characterized by its ancient beech and fir forests, notably the Irati Forest, which provides extensive trail networks. Riders encounter varied elevations, from valley floors where the Zatoya and Anduña rivers converge, to significant ascents within the mountainous landscape. This area features a mix of rugged paths and scenic forest tracks, catering to different mountain biking preferences. Best mountain bike trails around Ochagavía/Otsagabia The most popular mountain bike trail is Abodi Trail, a difficult 36.3 miles (58.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 33 minutes to complete. This route offers panoramic views of the Pyrenees and rugged terrain through the Irati Forest. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Roncesvalles-Sierra de Irati – circular a la Cascada del Cubo por Santzenbizkarra, a difficult 25.1 miles (40.4 km) path. This route leads towards the prominent Cascada del Cubo waterfall, offering scenic natural features. Local mountain bikers also love the Abodi MTB Trail, a 17.2 miles (27.7 km) trail leading through the Irati Forest, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Ochagavía/Otsagabia is defined by the dramatic landscapes of the Navarrese Pyrenees, the dense Irati Forest, and the scenic Salazar Valley. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate forest paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Ochagavía/Otsagabia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 30 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ochagavía/Otsagabia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Ochagavía/Otsagabia?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Ochagavía/Otsagabia, offering a variety of experiences within the Navarrese Pyrenees and the Irati Forest. These routes range from moderate to difficult, catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails for beginners in Ochagavía/Otsagabia?
A: While many trails in the region are rated moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy mountain bike route available. Most routes involve varied elevations and can be challenging, so it's best to check individual route details on komoot for suitability.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular mountain bike routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989540750/selva-de-irati-circular-desde-casas-de-irati-por-paxula-y-embalse-de-koixta-btt-irati" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Selva de Irati – circular desde Casas de Irati por Paxula y Embalse de Koixta (BTT Irati)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 34.8 km loop through the Irati Forest. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988542734/ochagavia-lakuaga-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ochagavía - Lakuaga Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 41.6 km route.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that pass by waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989554648/roncesvalles-sierra-de-irati-circular-a-la-cascada-del-cubo-por-santzenbizkarra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roncesvalles-Sierra de Irati – circular a la Cascada del Cubo por Santzenbizkarra&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 40.4 km mountain bike trail that leads towards the prominent Cascada del Cubo waterfall, offering scenic natural features.

Q: What kind of natural features and landmarks can I expect to see along the trails?
A: Mountain bike trails in Ochagavía/Otsagabia often traverse the ancient beech and fir forests of the Irati Forest and offer panoramic views of the Navarrese Pyrenees. You might encounter significant mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bagargui Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Larrau Pass&lt;/a&gt;, or even catch glimpses of summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2706541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic d'Orhy&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also characterized by rivers like the Zatoya and Anduña.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or points of interest near the mountain biking routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore cultural and historical attractions. The spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/963896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Holzarte Footbridge&lt;/a&gt;, overlooking the Olhadubi Gorge, is a notable highlight. The charming village of Ochagavía/Otsagabia itself, with its traditional architecture and medieval bridge, is also worth exploring.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Ochagavía/Otsagabia?
A: The mountain bike trails in Ochagavía/Otsagabia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the dramatic landscapes of the Navarrese Pyrenees and the dense Irati Forest.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Ochagavía/Otsagabia?
A: The region is ideal for mountain biking from spring through autumn. The Irati Forest and Pyrenean landscapes are particularly stunning during these seasons. Winter can bring snow and colder conditions, making some trails inaccessible or requiring specialized gear.

Q: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the mountain bike trails?
A: Ochagavía/Otsagabia is a charming mountain town with traditional architecture, offering amenities. Within the Irati Forest, you might find facilities like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3094689" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iraty Chalets&lt;/a&gt;, which can serve as a base or a place to refuel. The village itself provides options for cafes, pubs, and accommodation.

Q: What is the typical terrain like on the mountain bike trails?
A: The terrain varies significantly, from rugged paths and forest tracks within the Irati Forest to more challenging ascents in the mountainous Pyrenean landscape. Riders can expect a mix of surfaces, including dirt, gravel, and rocky sections, often with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, experienced riders will find plenty of challenging options. Many routes are rated 'difficult,' such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989579478/abodi-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abodi Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 58.5 km route with over 1300 meters of elevation gain through the Irati Forest, offering rugged terrain and panoramic views.
tips: This very picturesque and well-maintained road allows you to get from Ochagavía to the top of Lakuaga at 1,415 masl and from there you can go down to the town of Isaba on the Ezka river.

Picturesque typical village of this area of the Navarrese interior. In 1794 the town was invaded by French troops who burned a large part of it and it was later rebuilt around the 19th century to what can be seen today. Among its places to visit are the walk along the waterfront of the Anduña river, the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Muskilda and the Church of San Juan Evangelistas, as well as having a large number of connections to paths and tracks through the Pyrenees.

faqs.question: What skill level is required for the mountain bike route from Ochagavía?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a 'difficult' mountain bike ride, demanding very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Riders should be prepared for technically demanding, natural hiking trails that can be rough and stony, and may even require pushing their bike in some sections.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 'Vuelta desde Ochagavía a la zona sur de los Pirineos Navarros' is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area, allowing you to return to your starting point without needing a shuttle.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts from the picturesque village of Ochagavía, which serves as a gateway to many routes in the Navarrese Pyrenees. As a significant starting point, you can generally find parking within or near the village.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging, featuring a mix of forest tracks and mountain trails. You can expect rough, stony paths that are often not well-maintained and can change due to weather conditions. The route traverses compact forest masses at lower altitudes and deforested areas with extensive mountain pastures.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect stunning natural beauty, including impressive mountain views and dense forests. The route passes through areas near the expansive Irati Forest, one of Europe's largest beech and fir forests. You'll also see mountain pastures and traditional shepherd's huts.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Pyrenees offer a wild and unspoiled adventure, but given the challenging nature and potential for changing weather, late spring, summer, and early autumn are generally the best times. Winter conditions can make the trails impassable or significantly more difficult due to snow and ice.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails in the Navarrese Pyrenees, but it is always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to practice Leave No Trace principles by cleaning up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this area of the Navarrese Pyrenees?
faqs.answer: For general mountain biking on this route in the Navarrese Pyrenees, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required. However, always respect local regulations and private property signs, especially when passing through pastures or near settlements.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Senda Pirenaica - E32 and also touches upon the Otsagabia bidea, Carretil de Xabros, and Sarria bidea.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate protective gear (helmet, gloves), and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather. A GPS device or the komoot app with the downloaded tour is highly recommended for navigation.

faqs.question: Are there any specific highlights or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route itself is a highlight for its challenging mountain biking experience and stunning Pyrenean scenery. You'll pass through the area of the Ochagavía-Lakuaga Trail, and the charming village of Ochagavía serves as both a starting point and a cultural highlight with its traditional architecture.
